title: David Ballester
text:
David Ballester

M.S. Business Analytics | B.S. Statistics
Data Science • Machine Learning • Clinical Research • Business Analytics
I am an analytics and statistics professional passionate about using data to support evidence-based decision making in business and healthcare settings.
My experience includes clinical research, customer churn analysis, developer engagement analytics, statistical modeling, machine learning, forecasting, dashboard development, and data visualization.
I have worked on applied analytics projects with Oracle and NVIDIA, contributed to published clinical research in spine biomechanics, and enjoy building reproducible, data-driven solutions that turn complex data into meaningful insights.

title: David Ballester
text:
This page highlights research projects involving biostatistics, clinical research, statistical modeling, and applied data analysis.



Role: Biostatistician Research Intern
Organization: Luskin Orthopedic Institute
Tools: R, RStudio, Excel
Focus: Clinical Research, Biomechanics, Medical Devices, Biostatistics
Collaborated with researchers and physicians from UCLA and USC to investigate how design characteristics of cervical disc replacement devices influence fixation and long-term stability.
The project involved evaluating five cervical disc replacement devices and identifying which design features contributed most strongly to stability, reduced movement, and improved patient outcomes. Responsibilities included data collection, statistical analysis, visualization, and interpretation of complex biomechanical data.
Methods Used: Regression, t-tests, ANOVA, Taguchi’s Method, Data Visualization
Outcome: Published co-author in Spine (2025).


Wahbeh JM, Ballester DA, Ebramzadeh E, Sangiorgio SN. Design Factors Affecting the Fixation of Cervical Disc Replacements: A Biomechanical Analysis. Spine (Phila Pa 1976). 2025 Aug 1;50(15):E299-E307.




Tools: R, RStudio, GitHub
Focus: Health Economics, Regression Analysis, Statistical Modeling
Conducted a statistical analysis investigating the relationship between population median age and healthcare spending across countries worldwide.
The project explored how demographic characteristics influence healthcare expenditures and applied regression modeling techniques to identify meaningful trends within international health data.
Methods Used: Regression Analysis, Data Visualization, Statistical Communication


View GitHub Repository

title: David Ballester
text:
This page highlights technical, analytical, and industry projects involving data science, machine learning, statistical modeling, business analytics, and dashboard development.



Organization: Oracle
Role: Data Analytics Consultant
Tools: Python, R, Clustering, Survival Analysis, Dashboard Development
Analyzed customer usage and transaction data to identify key drivers of customer churn across multiple client segments.
Applied clustering and survival analysis techniques to develop personalized retention strategies and uncover high-risk customer groups. The project focused on transforming behavioral data into actionable business recommendations.
Skills: Customer Analytics, Survival Analysis, Clustering, Data Visualization, Dashboard Development
Outcome: Presented findings and strategic recommendations to Oracle leadership, including retention bundle offerings and AI-driven pricing initiatives.



Organization: NVIDIA
Role: Data Analytics Consultant
Tools: Python, R, Clustering, Interactive Dashboards
Analyzed large-scale developer activity data to identify engagement patterns, customer segmentation opportunities, and behavioral trends across NVIDIA’s developer ecosystem.
Developed developer-level and organization-level analytics dashboards to track lifecycle activity, engagement metrics, and retention patterns. Applied clustering techniques to identify distinct developer archetypes and organizational behaviors.
Skills: Machine Learning, Customer Segmentation, Dashboard Development, Behavioral Analytics, Data Visualization
Outcome: Presented insights and recommendations to NVIDIA leadership focused on developer retention, onboarding pathways, and high-value user identification.



Tools: R, RStudio, GitHub
Focus: Health Economics, Regression Analysis, Statistical Modeling
Conducted a statistical analysis investigating the relationship between population median age and healthcare spending across countries worldwide.
The project explored how demographic characteristics influence healthcare expenditures and applied regression modeling techniques to identify meaningful trends within international health data.
Skills: Regression Analysis, Data Visualization, Statistical Communication


View GitHub Repository




Status: In Development
Additional machine learning, forecasting, dashboard, and data science projects will be added here as they are completed.
Potential future topics include predictive modeling, forecasting, classification systems, web scraping projects, cloud analytics, and interactive data applications.
